title: elatine triandra subsp. americana
intro: Elatine triandra subsp. americana, commonly known as American waterwort, is a plant species that belongs to the Elatinaceae family and is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help alleviate symptoms of digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Elatine triandra subsp. americana is traditionally prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
